evidence-binding: n2-fix-618 3e2eaae86094

## Summary

Fixed three fail-open holes in chain-receipt greenness (issue #618):

1. `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` sync path (`runChainSync`): a signal-killed child
   (`status===null`, no `error`) previously fell through `(r.status != null) ? r.status : (r.error ? 1 : 0)`
   to a false-green `exitCode: 0`. Fixed with `if (r.status == null && (r.signal || r.error)) exitCode = 1;`
   and the signal name is now recorded (`_signal` -> receipt `signal` field).
2. `kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` async path (`runChainAsync`): `close(null, SIGKILL)` with
   `timedOut===false` previously fell through `timedOut ? 1 : ((code != null) ? code : 0)` to a
   false-green `0`. Fixed with `exitCode: (timedOut || code == null) ? 1 : code`, and the `close`
   handler now reads the `signal` arg and records it. The existing #608 timer-timeout path
   (`timedOut===true`) is UNCHANGED and still maps to `exitCode: 1`.
3. `kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` `--finalize-check`: an empty `chains: []` in an otherwise
   fresh/HEAD-bound receipt previously passed the gate (the `redChains` filter is vacuously empty
   over `[]`). Added a typed `chains_empty` refusal, mirroring the producer's own `no_chains` guard,
   checked BEFORE the red-chain filter (precedence: `chains_unverified > chains_stale > chains_empty
   > chains_red`).
4. `scripts/test-run-chains.js` is now wired into the `test:kaola-workflow:claude` npm chain in
   `package.json` (its 143 assertions, up from 133, now run on every claude-chain invocation).

Edited across editions: `scripts/kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` (canonical) + byte-identical copy at
`plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-run-chains.js` (COMMON_SCRIPTS) + hand-ported
rename-normalized `plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/scripts/kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-run-chains.js`
(verified via `renameNormalize` byte-comparison, see GREEN section). `scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js`
(canonical, a GENERATED_AGGREGATOR) regenerated into all three plugin-tree ports via
`node scripts/edition-sync.js --write` (no hand-editing of the forge copies).

`plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/scripts/test-{gitlab,gitea}-run-chains.js` were left UNCHANGED:
inspected both — they cover ONLY the #550 classifier-export / transient-retry path (G1/G2/G3: a
callable `isTransientFetchStderr`, a determinate-red chain, a transient-then-pass chain). Neither
exercises exitCode-mapping or signal handling at all, so there is no existing coverage to mirror the
new assertions against; both still pass unchanged (20/20 assertions each) since the fix is additive
(new `signal` field, corrected exitCode branch that these tests never hit).

## RED (against unfixed code — `git checkout --` reverted run-chains.js + plan-validator.js to HEAD,
     keeping only the new test-run-chains.js assertions)

```
$ node scripts/test-run-chains.js
FAIL: T25: green chain records signal: null (#618 additive field); got {"name":"claude","exitCode":0,"command":"npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude","duration_ms":167,"accepted_red":false,"accepted_red_issue":null,"attempts":1,"retried_transient":false,"timed_out":false}
FAIL: T26: a signal-killed chain (sync path) stays RED (non-zero overall exit)
FAIL: T26: sync-path signal death maps to exitCode 1 (never a false green); got {"name":"claude","exitCode":0,"command":"npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude","duration_ms":385,"accepted_red":false,"accepted_red_issue":null,"attempts":1,"retried_transient":false,"timed_out":false}
FAIL: T26: the signal name SIGKILL is recorded in the receipt entry; got undefined
FAIL: T27: a signal-killed chain (async/concurrent path) stays RED (non-zero overall exit)
FAIL: T27: async-path signal death maps to exitCode 1 (never a false green); got {"name":"claude","exitCode":0,"command":"npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude","duration_ms":169,"accepted_red":false,"accepted_red_issue":null,"attempts":1,"retried_transient":false,"timed_out":false}
FAIL: T27: the signal name SIGKILL is recorded in the receipt entry; got undefined
FAIL: T28: the EXTERNALLY signal-killed chain records timed_out: false + signal: SIGKILL — distinct from a timeout; got {"name":"codex","exitCode":0,"command":"npm run test:kaola-workflow:codex","duration_ms":299,"accepted_red":false,"accepted_red_issue":null,"attempts":1,"retried_transient":false,"timed_out":false}
FAIL: T29: --finalize-check refuses (non-zero exit) on a fresh, empty chains[] receipt; got status 0 stdout={"result":"pass","mode":"chain-receipt","checkedChanges":0,"chains":[]}
FAIL: T29: typed chains_empty refusal on an empty chains[] receipt; got {"result":"pass","mode":"chain-receipt","checkedChanges":0,"chains":[]}
run-chains tests FAILED (10 failures, 133 passed)
```

This confirms all three bugs live against unfixed code: T26/T27 show a self-SIGKILLed child chain
recording `exitCode:0` on both the sync and async dispatch paths (the false green); T28 shows the
external-signal case specifically (`timed_out:false`) distinct from the already-correct timer-kill
case; T29 shows `--finalize-check` returning `{result:"pass", chains:[]}` for a receipt with zero
verified chains.

## GREEN (fixed code restored)

```
$ node scripts/test-run-chains.js
run-chains tests passed (143 assertions)
```

Per-chain confirmation of the two new dispatch-path fixes and the finalize gate:
- T26 (sync signal-kill): exitCode 1, timed_out false, signal "SIGKILL" — PASS
- T27 (async/concurrent signal-kill): exitCode 1, timed_out false, signal "SIGKILL"; sibling chain
  still exitCode 0 in the same run — PASS
- T28 (timeout vs. external-signal side-by-side): timer-killed chain keeps timed_out:true (the #608
  path, unchanged); externally signal-killed chain records timed_out:false + signal:"SIGKILL" — PASS
- T29 (`--finalize-check` on empty chains[]): status 1, `{result:"refuse", reason:"chains_empty"}` — PASS

Cross-edition parity checks:
```
$ node scripts/validate-script-sync.js
OK: 24 common scripts, 25 byte-identical groups, 8 rename-normalized families, 1 config/hooks.json family, and 7 forge export-superset families in sync.

$ node scripts/edition-sync.js --check
edition-sync: 10 forge aggregator ports in rename-normalized parity with canonical.

$ node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-run-chains.js
gitlab run-chains tests passed (20 assertions)

$ node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-run-chains.js
gitea run-chains tests passed (20 assertions)
```

Chain-wiring confirmation — `scripts/test-run-chains.js` now runs inline inside
`npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude` (grepped from the full chain log):
```
run-chains tests passed (143 assertions)
...
Workflow walkthrough simulation passed
active-folders-field-parity tests passed (61 assertions)
```

All four cross-edition chains green (required — this diff touches the gitlab/gitea edition trees,
#307):
```
$ npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude   -> exit 0 (run-chains tests passed (143 assertions); Workflow walkthrough simulation passed)
$ npm run test:kaola-workflow:codex    -> exit 0 (Kaola-Workflow walkthrough simulation passed)
$ npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitlab   -> exit 0 (GitLab Codex workflow walkthrough simulation passed)
$ npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitea    -> exit 0 (Gitea Codex workflow walkthrough simulation passed)
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] A signal-killed chain child records a red entry (exitCode 1 + signal name) on both sync and
      async paths, with RED-first tests (T26, T27; T28 distinguishes the external-signal case from
      the already-correct #608 timer-timeout case).
- [x] `--finalize-check` refuses an empty `chains[]` receipt with a typed reason (`chains_empty`),
      with a RED-first test (T29).
- [x] `test-run-chains.js` runs in the claude npm chain (`package.json` `test:kaola-workflow:claude`).
